_description: Python dangerous pattern catalog — Kiwi auto-detect + gap-fill

patterns.title: eval() Usage — Code Injection Risk
patterns.good:
import ast
data = ast.literal_eval(row[4]) if row[4] else []
patterns.why: eval() executes arbitrary code. Use ast.literal_eval() or json.loads() for data parsing

patterns.title: exec() Usage — Arbitrary Code Execution
patterns.good: # Use restricted execution or AST-based evaluation
patterns.why: exec() runs arbitrary code strings, enabling code injection attacks

patterns.title: subprocess shell=True — Command Injection Risk
patterns.why: shell=True with unsanitized input enables command injection. Use shell=False with list args

patterns.title: os.system() — Use subprocess Instead
patterns.why: os.system() runs commands in a shell, enabling injection. Use subprocess with shell=False

patterns.title: Hardcoded Credentials in Python Source
patterns.why: Hardcoded credentials in source are exposed via version control

patterns.title: SQL Injection via String Formatting
patterns.why: String formatting in SQL enables injection. Use parameterized queries

patterns.title: pickle.load — Arbitrary Code Execution Risk
patterns.why: pickle can execute arbitrary code during deserialization. Use json for untrusted data

patterns.title: Dynamic __import__() — Use importlib Instead
patterns.why: __import__ with dynamic input enables arbitrary module loading; importlib is safer and more explicit

patterns.title: yaml.load() Without SafeLoader — Code Execution Risk
patterns.why: yaml.load() with default Loader can execute arbitrary Python objects

patterns.title: Hardcoded User Path — Not Portable
patterns.why: Hardcoded paths break on other machines. Use Path.home() or expanduser()

patterns.title: assert for Input Validation — Stripped in -O Mode
patterns.good:
if not isinstance(user_id, int):
    raise TypeError("user_id must be int")
patterns.why: assert statements are removed when Python runs with -O flag. Use if/raise for validation

patterns.title: Bare Except Swallowing Errors Silently
patterns.why: Swallowing all exceptions hides bugs. Log the error or re-raise

patterns.title: Path Tilde Expansion via str.replace — Use os.path.expanduser
patterns.why: Manual tilde replacement is fragile and not portable

patterns.title: tempfile Without Context Manager — Resource Leak
patterns.why: Temp files without context manager may not be cleaned up on exception

patterns.title: Mutable Default Argument — Shared State Bug
patterns.why: Mutable defaults are shared across calls, causing unexpected state bugs

patterns.title: open() Without Encoding — Platform-Dependent Behavior
patterns.why: Default encoding varies by OS. Always specify encoding for text files

patterns.title: Global Variable Mutation via global Statement
patterns.why: Global state makes testing and debugging harder. Use class or pass state explicitly

patterns.title: sys.path.insert — Fragile Import Hack
patterns.good: # Use proper package structure with setup.py/pyproject.toml
patterns.why: sys.path hacks make imports order-dependent and fragile across environments

patterns.title: Nested Import Inside Function Body
patterns.why: Nested imports hide dependencies and add overhead on each call unless intentionally lazy

patterns.title: MD5/SHA1 for Security — Use SHA256+
patterns.why: MD5 and SHA1 are cryptographically broken. Use SHA256+ for security-sensitive hashing

patterns.title: random Module for Security — Use secrets Instead
patterns.why: random module is predictable (PRNG). Use secrets module for security-sensitive values

patterns.title: print() for Debugging — Use logging Module
patterns.good:
import logging
logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
logger.debug("user=%s, data=%s", user_id, data)
patterns.why: print() debug statements are not filterable and often leak to production

patterns.title: requests.get/post Without Timeout — Hangs Forever
patterns.why: HTTP requests without timeout can hang indefinitely if server doesn't respond

patterns.title: requests verify=False — SSL Bypass
patterns.why: Disabling SSL verification exposes to man-in-the-middle attacks

patterns.title: DEBUG=True in Production Config
patterns.why: DEBUG=True exposes stack traces, internal paths, and sensitive data to users

patterns.title: Wildcard Import — Namespace Pollution
patterns.why: Wildcard imports pollute namespace and make it unclear where names come from

patterns.title: String Concatenation in Loop — Use join()
patterns.why: String concatenation in loops creates new string objects each iteration — O(n²)

patterns.title: raise Without Exception Type
patterns.why: Bare raise without context makes debugging harder. Consider logging before re-raise

patterns.title: datetime.now() Without Timezone — Ambiguous Timestamps
patterns.good:
from datetime import datetime, timezone
created_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
patterns.why: datetime.now() uses local timezone which varies by server. Use timezone-aware datetimes

patterns.title: Global Dict Mutation Without Lock — Thread Unsafe
patterns.good:
import threading
lock = threading.Lock()
def update(key, val):
    with lock:
        cache[key] = val
patterns.why: Concurrent dict mutation without locking causes race conditions and data corruption

patterns.title: chmod 0o777 — World-Writable Permissions
patterns.why: World-writable files can be modified by any user on the system

patterns.title: JWT Decode Without Verification
patterns.why: Skipping JWT verification allows forged tokens to be accepted

patterns.title: XML Parsing — XXE Vulnerability Risk
patterns.good:
from defusedxml.ElementTree import parse
tree = parse(user_uploaded_file)
patterns.why: Standard XML parsers are vulnerable to XXE (XML External Entity) attacks

patterns.title: Regex with Catastrophic Backtracking Risk
patterns.why: Nested quantifiers cause exponential backtracking — ReDoS on untrusted input

patterns.title: Logging Sensitive Data (password, token, secret)
patterns.why: Logging sensitive data exposes it in log files, monitoring, and error tracking

patterns.title: Unvalidated Redirect — Open Redirect Risk
patterns.why: Unvalidated redirects can be exploited for phishing by pointing to malicious sites

patterns.title: Client IP from X-Forwarded-For — Spoofable
patterns.good: ip = request.remote_addr  # or use trusted proxy config
patterns.why: X-Forwarded-For header can be spoofed by clients. Only trust behind a known proxy

patterns.title: Hardcoded IP Address — Use Config
patterns.why: Hardcoded IPs break across environments. Use environment variables or config files

patterns.title: File Write Without Flush — Data Loss on Crash
patterns.why: Buffered writes can be lost on crash. Flush critical data explicitly

patterns.title: signal.signal in Thread — Only Works in Main Thread
patterns.bad:
def worker():
    signal.signal(signal.SIGINT, handler)  # raises ValueError
patterns.why: signal.signal() raises ValueError if called from non-main thread
